title: Logisim Basics
summary: Learn the Logisim workflow for building, operating, testing, and saving simple digital circuits.
content_html:
Logisim is a simulator for designing and testing digital logic circuits. The typical workflow is to place input pins, gates, wires, and output LEDs on the canvas, then switch to operate mode and toggle the inputs.

Input pins represent binary switches. Output LEDs make the result visible. Wires connect component pins, and each wire carries the same logic value along its connected path. In the simulator, the power supply and ground wiring are handled for you.

When testing a Logisim circuit, change the inputs through every row of the truth table and compare the observed LED outputs with the expected Boolean result.

Build:  pins -> gates -> wires -> LED
Test:   toggle every input combination
Record: truth table + circuit screenshot
Save:   keep each lab circuit in a clear folder

For the first lab, the core tasks are to test simple AND, NOT, and XOR circuits, then design equality circuits that output 1 when two or three inputs are all the same.

exercises.title: Operate mode
exercises.prompt: In Logisim, what do you use operate mode for?

exercises.title: Output component
exercises.prompt: Which component is commonly used in the labs to show whether a circuit output is 0 or 1?

exercises.title: Testing method
exercises.prompt: What table should you fill in when checking all possible input combinations?

exercises.title: Wire behavior
exercises.prompt: In a Logisim circuit, what does a wire do?
options: Connects component pins and carries the same logic value along the connected path
Counts how many inputs are active
exercises.answer: Connects component pins and carries the same logic value along the connected path
